text.title: Distributed Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Distributed Patterns` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: For cross-service transactions, Saga (with compensating actions) MUST be the default over 2PC. 2PC requires explicit justification (single-shard DB, coordinator availability).
Every Saga step MUST ship its compensating action in the same PR as the happy path. Compensation-later = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Untested compensations fail at incident time.

rule.statement: Event publish from a transactional DB MUST go through an Outbox table; direct broker calls inside transactions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Direct broker calls inside transactions create dual-write inconsistency.

rule.statement: Retries MUST use exponential backoff + jitter; flat retries amplify cascading outages.
rule.rationale: Synchronised retries are a DDOS-self pattern.

rule.statement: External calls MUST be wrapped in a circuit breaker with declared half-open probe + thresholds.
rule.rationale: Unbounded retries on dead dependencies cause thread-pool exhaustion.

rule.statement: Mutating HTTP endpoints MUST accept an Idempotency-Key header; duplicate-key requests return prior result.
rule.rationale: Without idempotency, network-retry produces double charges / writes.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
